evals.expected_output: Three subagents dispatched in parallel, findings merged and triaged, changes applied, test suite run, final report with LOC delta emitted.
expectations: stinky-output/ directory created before any subagents are launched
detect_runner.py and measure_loc.py are called in Phase 0
All three subagents (bugs, simplify, arch) are launched simultaneously in Phase 1
stinky-output/findings-bugs.json exists and contains valid JSON after Phase 1
stinky-output/findings-simplify.json exists and contains valid JSON after Phase 1
stinky-output/findings-arch.json exists and contains valid JSON after Phase 1
Triage summary is printed before Phase 3 changes are applied
Test suite is run in Phase 4 and result is recorded
DONE: signal emitted with iteration count and LOC delta

evals.prompt: This code is getting pretty stinky, can you clean it up?
evals.expected_output: Skill triggered by casual 'stinky' + 'clean up' language; full workflow runs including at least one iteration with changes applied.
expectations: Codebase goal is read from README or project manifest before spawning agents
stinky-output/session.json is created with baseline_loc populated
At least one change is applied and the final LOC is measured
Final report shows starting vs ending LOC and changes-applied count

evals.expected_output: Skill does NOT trigger — code is explained without spawning subagents or modifying anything.
expectations: Response is a plain explanation of the code's purpose and structure
